---
title: "Embedded Costs by Subclass and HP Adoption"
---

Implements sections 2.3.3, 2.3.4, and 2.4 of
*Measuring Cross-Subsidization for Seasonal Heat Pump Rates: Cost-of-Service Methodology Analysis*.

Shows how **non-coincident peak (NCP)** and **share of embedded costs** by electrical subclass
(heating type) change as additional heat pump adoption varies from 0% to 10%.
Embedded-cost share for a subclass = subclass NCP / sum of all subclass NCPs.

### IMPORTS

```{python}
from buildstock_fetch.scenarios import uniform_adoption
from buildstock_fetch.mixed_upgrade import MixedUpgradeScenario
from cloudpathlib import S3Path
import polars as pl
from pathlib import Path
```

### PARAMETERS

```{python}
state = "RI"
release = "res_2024_amy2018_2"
data_path = S3Path("s3://data.sb/nrel/resstock/")
random_seed = 67
upgrade_id = 2

force_recrunch = True
DEBUG_MODE = False

# for lining up with prior RIE data
normalize_secondary_to_rie = True
use_rie_primary_to_secondary_adjustment_factor = True

if DEBUG_MODE:
    sample_n = 20 # NOTE: DEBUG MODE: sample only 5 homes
    step_size = 0.25
    num_steps = 2
    embedded_cost_total_dollars *= sample_n / 1910 # downscale so dollar estimtes are reasonable
else:
    sample_n = None
    step_size = 0.01
    num_steps = 10



# Additional HP adoption: 0 = current stock only; sweep from 0 to 10% in 1% steps
additional_adoption_pct_values = [round(x * step_size, 2) for x in range(num_steps + 1)]  # 0.00, 0.01, ..., 0.10

# Total embedded transmission + primary distribution costs (dollars)

# If True, ignore any cached aggregate_curves parquet files and recompute from scratch
```

### READ DATA FROM GOOGLE SHEET

```{python}
import os
import gspread
from dotenv import load_dotenv

# Load variables from .env
load_dotenv()

# Reconstruct the credential dictionary
app_creds = {
    "installed": {
        "client_id": os.getenv("G_CLIENT_ID"),
        "client_secret": os.getenv("G_CLIENT_SECRET"),
        "project_id": os.getenv("G_PROJECT_ID"),
        "auth_uri": os.getenv("G_AUTH_URI"),
        "token_uri": os.getenv("G_TOKEN_URI"),
        "redirect_uris": ["http://localhost"],
    }
}

# This will trigger the browser login for the user
gc, authorized_user = gspread.oauth_from_dict(credentials=app_creds)
```

```{python}
spreadsheet_url = "https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1JtoKPimoyP88TIZGIKP9vYuPfaY5-Ds14GF9lTWmIt8/edit?gid=0#gid=0"
sh = gc.open_by_url(spreadsheet_url)


def worksheet_to_polars_df(ws):
    # Get all rows, drop fully empty rows, and use the first remaining row as header
    values = ws.get_all_values()
    values = [row for row in values if any(cell.strip() for cell in row)]
    if not values:
        return pl.DataFrame()

    header, *data_rows = values
    n_cols = len(header)

    # Ensure each data row has the same number of columns as the header
    cleaned_rows = [
        (row[:n_cols] + [""] * (n_cols - len(row))) if len(row) < n_cols else row[:n_cols]
        for row in data_rows
    ]

    return pl.DataFrame(cleaned_rows, schema=header)


allocators_ws = sh.worksheet("allocators")
revenue_requirement_ws = sh.worksheet("revenue_requirement")

allocators_df = worksheet_to_polars_df(allocators_ws)
revenue_requirement_df = worksheet_to_polars_df(revenue_requirement_ws)

print("Allocators sheet:")
print(allocators_df)
print("\nRevenue requirement sheet:")
print(revenue_requirement_df)
```

### HELPER FUNCTIONS
```{python}
def _get_heating_type_distribution(sbmeta: pl.LazyFrame) -> pl.DataFrame:
    """Heating type counts and percent of total (by weight). Collects only the aggregation."""
    return (
        sbmeta.group_by("postprocess_group.heating_type")
        .agg(pl.col("weight").sum().alias("total_homes"))
        .collect()
        .with_columns((pl.col("total_homes") / pl.col("total_homes").sum()).alias("pct_of_total"))
    )


def _merge_weights(
    ldf: pl.LazyFrame, metadata: pl.LazyFrame
) -> pl.LazyFrame:
    return ldf.join(
        metadata.select(["bldg_id", "weight", "postprocess_group.heating_type"]),
        on="bldg_id",
        how="left",
    )


def read_mixed_upgrade_scenario(
    scenario: dict[int, list[float]],
    scenario_name: str,
    baseline_metadata: pl.LazyFrame,
    data_path: S3Path,
    release: str,
    state: str,
    random_seed: int | None = None,
    sample_n: int | None = None,
):
    """Run mixed-upgrade scenario and return metadata and weighted load curves."""
    mus = MixedUpgradeScenario(
        scenario_name=scenario_name,
        data_path=data_path,
        release=release,
        states=state,
        random=random_seed,
        sample_n=sample_n,
        scenario=scenario,
    )
    mus_metadata = mus.read_metadata()
    sbmeta = (
        baseline_metadata.join(
            mus_metadata.rename({"upgrade_id": "upgrade_mus"}),
            on="bldg_id",
            how="left",
        )
        .with_columns(
            pl.col("upgrade_mus").fill_null(pl.col("upgrade")).alias("upgrade")
        )
        .drop("upgrade_mus")
        .with_columns(
            pl.when(pl.col("upgrade") > 0)
            .then(pl.lit("heat_pump"))
            .otherwise(pl.col("postprocess_group.heating_type"))
            .alias("postprocess_group.heating_type")
        )
    )
    ldf = mus.read_load_curve_hourly().pipe(
        lambda ldf: _merge_weights(ldf, metadata=sbmeta)
    )
    return mus, sbmeta, ldf
```

### LOAD BASELINE METADATA

```{python}
metadata_path = (
    data_path / release / "metadata" / f"state={state}" / "upgrade=00" / "metadata-sb.parquet"
)
if metadata_path.exists():
    baseline_sbmeta = pl.scan_parquet(str(metadata_path))
else:
    path_plain = (
        data_path / release / "metadata" / f"state={state}" / "upgrade=00" / "metadata.parquet"
    )
    baseline_sbmeta = pl.scan_parquet(str(path_plain)).with_columns(
        pl.when(
            pl.any_horizontal(
                [
                    pl.col("in.hvac_heating_type_and_fuel").str.contains(s, literal=True)
                    for s in ("MSHP", "ASHP", "GSHP")
                ]
            )
        )
        .then(pl.lit("heat_pump"))
        .otherwise(pl.lit("non-heat_pump"))
        .alias("postprocess_group.heating_type")
    )

# Ensure weight column exists (hack until weight is in metadata). Keep baseline as LazyFrame.
if "weight" not in baseline_sbmeta.collect_schema().names():
    baseline_sbmeta = baseline_sbmeta.with_columns(pl.lit(252.301639).alias("weight"))

# Current HP share (for scaling adoption trajectory). Collect only the aggregation.
heating_dist = _get_heating_type_distribution(baseline_sbmeta)
current_hp_pct = (
    heating_dist.filter(pl.col("postprocess_group.heating_type") == "heat_pump")
    .select("pct_of_total")
    .item()
)
print(f"Baseline heat pump share (by weight): {current_hp_pct:.2%}")
```

### SWEEP ADDITIONAL HP ADOPTION AND COMPUTE NCP / SHARES

For each additional HP adoption percent (0 to 10%), we run the mixed-upgrade scenario,
aggregate load by subclass (heating type), compute non-coincident peak (NCP) per subclass,
total NCP, coincident peak, and each subclass’s share of total NCP.

```{python}
def compute_ncp_and_shares(
    aggregate_curves: pl.DataFrame,
) -> tuple[pl.DataFrame, float, float, pl.Series]:
    """
    From timestamp x heating_type aggregated load (electricity_kwh, num_homes),
    return (ncp_by_subclass, total_residential_ncp, coincident_peak_kw, coincident_peak_timestamp).
    """
    # NCP per subclass = max load over time for that subclass
    ncp_by_subclass = (
        aggregate_curves.group_by("postprocess_group.heating_type")
        .agg(
            pl.col("electricity_kwh").max().alias("ncp_kw"),
            pl.col("num_homes").max().alias("num_homes"),
        )
    )
    total_residential_ncp = ncp_by_subclass["ncp_kw"].sum()
    ncp_by_subclass = ncp_by_subclass.with_columns(
        # Subclass share of the residential class NCP (percent)
        (pl.col("ncp_kw") / total_residential_ncp * 100).alias("subclass_pct_of_class_share"),
        # Each home's share of total residential NCP (sums to 1 across all homes)
        (pl.col("ncp_kw") / total_residential_ncp / pl.col("num_homes")).alias(
            "share_of_residential_ncp_per_home"
        ),
    )

    # Coincident peak = max over time of total system load
    total_load_by_time = aggregate_curves.group_by("timestamp").agg(
        pl.col("electricity_kwh").sum().alias("total_kw")
    )
    idx_max = total_load_by_time["total_kw"].arg_max()
    peak_row = total_load_by_time.row(idx_max, named=True)
    coincident_peak_kw = peak_row["total_kw"]
    coincident_peak_timestamp = peak_row["timestamp"]

    return ncp_by_subclass, total_residential_ncp, coincident_peak_kw, coincident_peak_timestamp
```

```{python}
# Collect results for all adoption levels
rows_ncp = []
rows_system = []
aggregate_curves_cache_dir = Path("cache") / "embedded_curves"
aggregate_curves_cache_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

for additional_adoption_pct in additional_adoption_pct_values:
    # Scale adoption so that *additional* HP adoption (on top of current) equals additional_adoption_pct
    pct_in_trajectory = (
        additional_adoption_pct / (1 - current_hp_pct) if current_hp_pct < 1.0 else 0.0
    )

    cache_file = aggregate_curves_cache_dir / f"aggregate_curves_{additional_adoption_pct:.2f}.parquet"

    if cache_file.exists() and not force_recrunch:
        aggregate_curves = pl.read_parquet(cache_file)
    else:
        scenario = uniform_adoption(
            upgrade_ids=[upgrade_id],
            weights={upgrade_id: 1.0},
            adoption_trajectory=[pct_in_trajectory],
        )
        mus, sbmeta, ldf_load_curves = read_mixed_upgrade_scenario(
            scenario,
            f"additional_adoption_{additional_adoption_pct:.2f}",
            baseline_sbmeta,
            data_path,
            release,
            state,
            random_seed=random_seed,
            sample_n=sample_n,
        )

        # Aggregate load by timestamp and heating type (subclass)
        load_with_meta = ldf_load_curves.join(
            sbmeta.select(["bldg_id", "postprocess_group.heating_type", "weight"]),
            on="bldg_id",
            how="left",
        )
        aggregate_curves = (
            load_with_meta.group_by(["timestamp", "postprocess_group.heating_type"])
            .agg(
                (pl.col("out.electricity.net.energy_consumption") * pl.col("weight"))
                .sum()
                .alias("electricity_kwh"),
                pl.col("weight").sum().alias("num_homes"),
            )
            .collect()
        )
        aggregate_curves.write_parquet(cache_file)

    # Annual kWh by subclass and total kWh across all subclasses
    annual_kwh_by_subclass = aggregate_curves.group_by(
        "postprocess_group.heating_type"
    ).agg(pl.col("electricity_kwh").sum().alias("annual_kwh"))
    total_annual_kwh = annual_kwh_by_subclass["annual_kwh"].sum()

    ncp_by_subclass, total_residential_ncp, coincident_peak_kw, coincident_peak_timestamp = (
        compute_ncp_and_shares(aggregate_curves)
    )

    # Attach annual kWh to NCP table
    ncp_by_subclass = ncp_by_subclass.join(
        annual_kwh_by_subclass,
        on="postprocess_group.heating_type",
        how="left",
    )

    # Total HP adoption (fraction of homes) at this additional adoption level
    total_num_homes = ncp_by_subclass["num_homes"].sum()
    hp_num_homes = (
        ncp_by_subclass.filter(
            pl.col("postprocess_group.heating_type") == "heat_pump"
        )["num_homes"].sum()
        if "heat_pump" in ncp_by_subclass["postprocess_group.heating_type"].to_list()
        else 0.0
    )
    total_hp_adoption_pct = hp_num_homes / total_num_homes if total_num_homes > 0 else 0.0

    for r in ncp_by_subclass.iter_rows(named=True):
        rows_ncp.append(
            {
                "additional_adoption_pct": additional_adoption_pct,
                "postprocess_group.heating_type": r["postprocess_group.heating_type"],
                "ncp_kw": r["ncp_kw"],
                "num_homes": r["num_homes"],
                "subclass_pct_of_class_share": r["subclass_pct_of_class_share"],
                "share_of_residential_ncp_per_home": r["share_of_residential_ncp_per_home"],
                "annual_kwh": r["annual_kwh"],
                "total_hp_adoption_pct": total_hp_adoption_pct,
            }
        )
    rows_system.append(
        {
            "additional_adoption_pct": additional_adoption_pct,
            "total_residential_ncp_kw": total_residential_ncp,
            "coincident_peak_kw": coincident_peak_kw,
            "coincident_peak_timestamp": coincident_peak_timestamp,
            "total_annual_kwh": total_annual_kwh,
            "total_hp_adoption_pct": total_hp_adoption_pct,
        }
    )

df_ncp = pl.DataFrame(rows_ncp)
df_system = pl.DataFrame(rows_system)

# Embedded-cost reallocation based on coincident peak.
# Baseline coincident peak corresponds to residential class paying 49% of embedded costs.
baseline_row = df_system.filter(
    pl.col("additional_adoption_pct") == 0.0
).select("coincident_peak_kw")
baseline_coincident_peak_kw = baseline_row["coincident_peak_kw"][0]

df_system = df_system.with_columns(
    (
        pl.col("coincident_peak_kw")
        / (
            baseline_coincident_peak_kw / 0.49
            + pl.col("coincident_peak_kw")
            - baseline_coincident_peak_kw
        )
        * 100
    ).alias("residential_embedded_cost_share_pct"),
    (
        pl.col("coincident_peak_kw")
        / (
            baseline_coincident_peak_kw / 0.49
            + pl.col("coincident_peak_kw")
            - baseline_coincident_peak_kw
        )
        / pl.col("total_annual_kwh")
        * embedded_cost_total_dollars
    ).alias(
        "volumetric_charge_for_transmission_and_primary_distribution_per_kwh"
    ),
)

# Join system-level residential embedded-cost share into subclass table and
# compute each subclass's share and per-kWh charge.
df_ncp = df_ncp.join(
    df_system.select(
        [
            "additional_adoption_pct",
            "total_residential_ncp_kw",
            "residential_embedded_cost_share_pct",
        ]
    ),
    on="additional_adoption_pct",
    how="left",
)

df_ncp = df_ncp.with_columns(
    # Subclass share of total embedded costs (fraction of systemwide embedded costs)
    (
        (pl.col("subclass_pct_of_class_share") / 100.0)
        * pl.col("residential_embedded_cost_share_pct")
    ).alias("subclass_pct_of_total_embedded_cost"),
    # Per-home share of total embedded costs
    (
        (pl.col("subclass_pct_of_class_share") / 100.0)
        * pl.col("residential_embedded_cost_share_pct")
        / 100.0
        / pl.col("num_homes")
    ).alias("subclass_embedded_cost_share_per_home"),
    # Dollar share of embedded costs for each subclass
    (
        (pl.col("subclass_pct_of_class_share") / 100.0)
        * pl.col("residential_embedded_cost_share_pct")
        / 100.0
        * embedded_cost_total_dollars
    ).alias("subclass_embedded_cost_dollars"),
    # Per-kWh charge for each subclass
    (
        (pl.col("subclass_pct_of_class_share") / 100.0)
        * pl.col("residential_embedded_cost_share_pct")
        / 100.0
        * embedded_cost_total_dollars
        / pl.col("annual_kwh")
    ).alias("subclass_per_kwh_charge"),
)
```

### NCP AND EMBEDDED-COST SHARE AT ZERO ADDITIONAL ADOPTION

```{python}
# Subclass NCP and share of total NCP when additional HP adoption = 0%
at_zero = df_ncp.filter(pl.col("additional_adoption_pct") == 0.0).sort(
    "ncp_kw", descending=True
)
print("At 0% additional HP adoption:")
print(at_zero)
```

### TOTAL PEAK LOAD VS ADDITIONAL HP ADOPTION

```{python}
print("System-level peaks vs additional HP adoption:")
print(df_system)
```

### HEAT PUMP SUBCLASS: NCP AND SHARE OF TOTAL NCP VS ADOPTION

```{python}
hp_only = df_ncp.filter(pl.col("postprocess_group.heating_type") == "heat_pump")
print("Heat pump subclass NCP and share of total NCP:")
print(hp_only.to_pandas().transpose())
```

### PLOTS

```{python}
from plotnine import (
    ggplot,
    aes,
    geom_line,
    geom_point,
    geom_area,
    labs,
    theme_minimal,
)
```

**Total system peak (NCP sum and coincident peak) vs additional HP adoption**

```{python}
# Long form for ggplot: one row per (adoption_pct, series, value)
df_system_long = df_system.melt(
    id_vars="total_hp_adoption_pct",
    value_vars=["total_residential_ncp_kw", "coincident_peak_kw"],
    variable_name="series",
    value_name="peak_kw",
).with_columns(
    pl.when(pl.col("series") == "total_residential_ncp_kw")
    .then(pl.lit("Sum of residential NCPs"))
    .otherwise(pl.lit("Coincident peak"))
    .alias("series"),
)

(
    ggplot(
        df_system_long,
        aes(x="total_hp_adoption_pct", y="peak_kw", color="series"),
    )
    + geom_line(size=1)
    + geom_point(size=2)
    + labs(
        title="Total system peak vs additional HP adoption",
        x="Total HP adoption (fraction of homes)",
        y="Peak load (kW)",
        color="",
    )
    + theme_minimal()
).draw()
```

```{python}
print(df_system_long.to_pandas())
```

**NCP by subclass (stacked area) vs additional HP adoption**

```{python}
(
    ggplot(
        df_ncp.to_pandas(),
        aes(
            x="total_hp_adoption_pct",
            y="ncp_kw",
            fill="postprocess_group.heating_type",
        ),
    )
    + geom_area(position="stack")
    + labs(
        title="NCP by subclass vs additional HP adoption",
        x="Total HP adoption (fraction of homes)",
        y="NCP (kW)",
        fill="Subclass",
    )
    + theme_minimal()
).draw()
```

**Heat pump share of total NCP (%) vs additional HP adoption**

```{python}
(
    ggplot(
        hp_only.to_pandas(),
        aes(x="total_hp_adoption_pct", y="subclass_pct_of_class_share"),
    )
    + geom_line(size=1)
    + geom_point(size=2)
    + labs(
        title="Heat pump share of total NCP vs additional HP adoption",
        x="Total HP adoption (fraction of homes)",
        y="HP share of total NCP (%)",
    )
    + theme_minimal()
).draw()
```

**All subclasses: share of total NCP vs additional HP adoption**

```{python}
(
    ggplot(
        df_ncp.to_pandas(),
        aes(
            x="total_hp_adoption_pct",
            y="subclass_pct_of_class_share",
            color="postprocess_group.heating_type",
        ),
    )
    + geom_line(size=1)
    + geom_point(size=2)
    + labs(
        title="Subclass share of total NCP vs additional HP adoption",
        x="Additional HP adoption (fraction)",
        y="Share of total NCP (%)",
        color="Subclass",
    )
    + theme_minimal()
).draw()
```

### Embedded-cost reallocation plots

**Total residential embedded costs vs total HP adoption (stacked by subclass)**

```{python}
(
    ggplot(
        df_ncp.to_pandas(),
        aes(
            x="total_hp_adoption_pct",
            y="subclass_embedded_cost_dollars",
            fill="postprocess_group.heating_type",
        ),
    )
    + geom_area(position="stack")
    + labs(
        title="Total residential embedded costs vs total HP adoption",
        x="Total HP adoption (fraction of homes)",
        y="Embedded transmission + primary distribution costs (dollars)",
        fill="Subclass",
    )
    + theme_minimal()
).draw()
```

**Embedded-cost per kWh vs total HP adoption (by subclass)**

```{python}
(
    ggplot(
        df_ncp.to_pandas(),
        aes(
            x="total_hp_adoption_pct",
            y="subclass_per_kwh_charge",
            color="postprocess_group.heating_type",
        ),
    )
    + geom_line(size=1)
    + geom_point(size=2)
    + labs(
        title="Embedded transmission + primary distribution cost per kWh vs total HP adoption",
        x="Total HP adoption (fraction of homes)",
        y="Embedded cost per kWh (dollars)",
        color="Subclass",
    )
    + theme_minimal()
).draw()
```

**Embedded-cost per home vs total HP adoption (by subclass)**

```{python}
df_ncp_with_per_home = df_ncp.with_columns(
    (
        pl.col("subclass_embedded_cost_dollars") / pl.col("num_homes")
    ).alias("subclass_embedded_cost_dollars_per_home")
)

(
    ggplot(
        df_ncp_with_per_home.to_pandas(),
        aes(
            x="total_hp_adoption_pct",
            y="subclass_embedded_cost_dollars_per_home",
            color="postprocess_group.heating_type",
        ),
    )
    + geom_line(size=1)
    + geom_point(size=2)
    + labs(
        title="Embedded transmission + primary distribution cost per home vs total HP adoption",
        x="Total HP adoption (fraction of homes)",
        y="Embedded cost per home (dollars)",
        color="Subclass",
    )
    + theme_minimal()
).draw()
```

# Debugging: Plot total electric load over time for aggregate curve with additional_hp_pct = 0https://accounts.google.com/SignOutOptions?hl=en&continue=https://drive.google.com/file/d/1qrhdnzkRx503QVnHciothIABWZbMhIJt/view&service=writely&ec=GBRAGQ
```{python}
# Read from the cache: aggregate curve with additional_hp_pct = 0
import polars as pl
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

additional_adoption_pct = 0.0

aggregate_curves_cache_dir = Path("cache") / "embedded_curves"
cache_path = aggregate_curves_cache_dir / f"aggregate_curves_{additional_adoption_pct:.2f}.parquet"

agg_curve_0 = pl.read_parquet(cache_path)

# Group by timestamp and sum electricity_kwh
total_load_by_time = (
    agg_curve_0.group_by("timestamp")
    .agg(pl.col("electricity_kwh").sum().alias("total_kw"))
    .sort("timestamp")
)

# Plot the total load over time
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 5))
plt.plot(
    total_load_by_time["timestamp"].to_pandas(),
    total_load_by_time["total_kw"].to_pandas()
)
plt.title("Total load over time (additional_hp_pct = 0)")
plt.xlabel("Timestamp")
plt.ylabel("Total kWh")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.grid(True)
plt.show()
```

```{python}
from buildstock_fetch.read import BuildStockRead

# Load hourly curves for upgrade 0 for state=RI
bsr = BuildStockRead(data_path=data_path, release=release, states=state)
df = bsr.read_load_curve_hourly(upgrades=[0])

if "weight" not in df.collect_schema().names():
    df = df.with_columns(pl.lit(252.301639).alias("weight"))

# Group by timestamp and sum the relevant columns
hourly_sum = (
    df
    .group_by("timestamp")
    .agg([
        pl.col("weight") * pl.col("out.electricity.net.energy_consumption").sum(),
        pl.col("weight") * pl.col("out.electricity.total.energy_consumption").sum()
    ])
    .collect()
)
```

```{python}
from plotnine import ggplot, aes, geom_line, labs, theme, element_text, scale_color_manual

# Prepare data for plotnine (long format)
# Melt Polars DataFrame to long format, map names, and keep all in Polars
plot_df = hourly_sum.rename({
    "out.electricity.net.energy_consumption": "Net Electricity Consumption",
    "out.electricity.total.energy_consumption": "Total Electricity Consumption"
}).melt(
    id_vars=["timestamp"],
    variable_name="Consumption Type",
    value_name="Electricity Consumption (kWh)"
)

# Plot using ggplot (plotnine)
(
    ggplot(plot_df, aes(x="timestamp", y="Electricity Consumption (kWh)", color="Consumption Type"))
    + geom_line()
    + labs(
        title="Net and Total Electricity Consumption Over Time (Upgrade 0, RI)",
        x="Timestamp",
        y="Electricity Consumption (kWh)",
        color=""
    )
    + theme(
        figure_size=(14, 5),
        axis_text_x=element_text(rotation=30, ha='right')
    )
    + scale_color_manual(
        values=["#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e"]
    )
).draw()

```

```{python}
print(df.limit(20).collect())
```
